params.stem: A store shelf has 4 packs of juice boxes. Each pack has 6 juice boxes.
params.description: Text scenario appears: A store shelf has 4 packs of juice boxes. Each pack has 6 juice boxes.

beats.text: Equal groups aren't just in math problems. Here's a real situation.

elements.description: Text scenario displayed: A store shelf has 4 packs of juice boxes. Each pack has 6 juice boxes.

params.description: Equal groups building interface appears. Empty workspace ready for student to set container count.

beats.text: Can you build what the shelf looks like?
How many groups? Set the packs.

elements.description: Text scenario: A store shelf has 4 packs of juice boxes. Each pack has 6 juice boxes.
Equal groups in building mode. 4 empty containers displayed. Ready for items per container.

beats.text: How many in each pack? Add the juice boxes.
How many in each pack? Add the juice boxes.

validator.description: Student set 6 items per container, correct

beats.text: 4 packs, 6 in each. Equal groups — right there on a store shelf.

elements.description: Text scenario: A store shelf has 4 packs of juice boxes. Each pack has 6 juice boxes.
Equal groups showing 4 containers, each containing 6 items. Complete representation of the juice box scenario.

beats.text: 4 groups of 6. That's 4 × 6. Any time you see the same number in each container — packs, bags, boxes, cartons — that's equal groups. It's multiplication, even outside math class.

elements.description: Text scenario: A store shelf has 4 packs of juice boxes. Each pack has 6 juice boxes.
Equal groups showing 4 containers, each containing 6 items. Represents the completed real-world scenario.
